evals.name: darken-muted-text
evals.prompt: The muted text on our cards feels a little too light — can you darken it a bit so it's easier to read?
evals.expected_output: Identifies muted-foreground as the token, measures contrast against muted with the checker, darkens it to clear 4.5:1 in BOTH :root and .dark, leaves locked brand tokens untouched, re-verifies.

assertions.text: Edits the --muted-foreground token (not muted, not an unrelated token)
Uses oklch format for the new value
Changes the value in both :root and .dark (or explicitly verifies dark already passes)
Runs the contrast checker / reports a measured ratio rather than guessing
Resulting muted-foreground/muted contrast is >= 4.5:1 in light mode
Does not modify any locked brand token (--primary, --chart-*)

evals.name: add-brand-blue-token
evals.prompt: We got a new brand accent color, #1d9bf0. Add it as a token called brand-blue and wire it up so I can use bg-brand-blue in components.
evals.expected_output: Converts #1d9bf0 to oklch, adds --brand-blue to both :root and .dark, adds --color-brand-blue: var(--brand-blue) to the @theme inline block so Tailwind generates the utility, and checks contrast for likely usage.

assertions.text: Adds --color-brand-blue: var(--brand-blue) inside the @theme inline block
Defines the token in both :root and .dark
Runs a contrast check for the new color against a plausible text/background pairing
Does not alter the locked brand tokens or other unrelated tokens

evals.name: audit-palette-accessibility
evals.prompt: Is our color palette accessible enough for the schools we sell to? They require WCAG AA.
evals.expected_output: Runs the audit across light and dark, reports measured ratios per pair, correctly flags real text/non-text failures while applying interpretation (decorative borders exempt, large-text caveat, color-alone for status colors). Does not change any tokens unprompted.

assertions.text: Runs the bundled checker in audit mode against globals.css
Reports results for BOTH light and dark themes
Cites the correct AA thresholds (4.5:1 text, 3:1 large/non-text)
Flags at least the genuine failures (e.g. muted-foreground light, sidebar-primary-foreground)
Applies interpretation rather than blindly listing every fail (notes decorative border exemption or large-text/color-alone nuance)
Does not edit any token values (audit was read-only request)
